articles.title: Falcons rookie James Pearce Jr. avoids jail after explosive breakup drama with WNBA star Rickea Jackson
articles.description: Atlanta Falcons player James Pearce Jr. has secured a favorable legal outcome. A Florida court approved him for a program that will erase his record upon completion. This comes after an incident involving his ex-girlfriend, WNBA star Rickea Jackson. Pearce must complete a year-long program with specific conditions. Meanwhile, the NFL's personal conduct policy investigation into the matter is ongoing.

articles.title: Rajat Patidar walks the talk with a six-hitting masterclass in Dharamsala
articles.description: Rajat Patidar backed his pre-match claim of preferring pace over spin with a breathtaking 93 off 33 balls against Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1. The RCB skipper smashed nine sixes -- seven against pacers -- and now has 41 sixes this IPL season, with 27 coming against fast bowlers. Batting at No. 4, Patidar has scored 486 runs in 14 matches at a strike rate of 196.76, underlining his impact as one of RCB's most destructive middle-order batters.

articles.title: Revisiting Three Biggest Controversies In Kapil Dev's Legendary Cricket Career
articles.description:
Kapil Dev is widely universally celebrated as one of the greatest all-rounders in cricket history and the legendary captain who led India to its historic 1983 World Cup triumph. However, his journey through Indian cricket was not entirely smooth sailing. Across his nearly two-decade-long career and his subsequent stint as a coach, he found himself at the center of several high-profile controversies.

Here is a report detailing the top 3 controversies of Kapil Dev's career:

2000 Match-Fixing Allegations

The absolute biggest storm of Kapil Dev's professional life arrived in May 2000. Former teammate Manoj Prabhakar accused Kapil of offering him a bribe of ₹25 lakh to underperform or throw away an ODI match against Pakistan during the 1994 Singer Cup in Sri Lanka.

Also Read | RCB Star Announces Retirement Hours Before IPL 2026 Qualifier 1 Against GT

The allegation sent shockwaves through the nation, deeply denting Kapil's image while he was serving as the head coach of the Indian national team.

In a highly emotional, live televised interview with journalist Karan Thapar on BBC, Kapil broke down in tears, famously stating that he would rather commit suicide than betray his country. Following an intense investigation, India's Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) completely exonerated and cleared Kapil Dev of all charges due to a lack of substantial evidence.

Cold War with Sunil Gavaskar

Throughout the 1980s, Indian cricket was reportedly heavily divided into two factions - one backing the classical, conservative batting maestro Sunil Gavaskar, and the other backing the explosive, flamboyant Kapil Dev. BCCI frequently rotated the national captaincy back and forth between the two stalwarts, which naturally strained their personal and professional dynamic.

Also on ABP Live | Watch: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Reacts As Riyan Parag Declines Photo Request

While both legends maintained mutual respect on the surface, the dressing room atmosphere remained highly tense during transition phases, establishing one of the most talked-about player rivalries in Indian sports history.

Infamous 1984 Eden Gardens Dropping

The friction between Gavaskar and Kapil reached a boiling point during England's 1984-85 tour of India. During the second Test match in Delhi, Kapil played a highly aggressive, rash stroke to get dismissed, a move that critics believed triggered a dramatic Indian batting collapse and handed England the victory.

In an unprecedented move, Kapil was completely axed from the playing XI for the subsequent third Test at Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

The public was furious. Angry fans heavily targeted skipper Sunil Gavaskar, chanting "No Kapil, No Test!" and throwing vegetables at the captain. Gavaskar later clarified that he did not actively propose axing Kapil, pointing the blame entirely toward the selection committee led by Chandu Borde.

articles.title: SAFF Championship: India women score one shy of a dozen
articles.description: India dominated Maldives with an 11-0 victory in their SAFF Women's Championship opener. Despite the resounding scoreline, coach Crispin Chettri expressed dissatisfaction with the team's second-half performance, emphasizing the need to convert chances more effectively. Aveka Singh shone with four goals, while Priyangka Devi Naorem and Karishma Shirvoikar also contributed significantly to the dominant win.

articles.title: Delhi Gymkhana Club row: Centre tells HC no forcible takeover before June 5, alternate accommodation possible
articles.description: The Centre has assured the Delhi High Court that the Delhi Gymkhana Club premises will not be forcibly taken over before June 5. The government stated that the club has been given the option to vacate voluntarily. Alternate accommodation may also be provided as part of the lease determination process. The court heard pleas related to the club dispute.

articles.title: Arshdeep Singh's Instagram detox: Changes DP, deletes 200+ Instagram posts; Viral Virat Kohli reel also removed
articles.description: India pacer Arshdeep Singh has sparked speculation after deleting over 200 Instagram posts and changing his display picture, reducing his profile to just 40 posts. The social media cleanup also saw the removal of his viral reel with Virat Kohli, which had garnered millions of views online.

articles.title: Conor McGregor contract update: The Notorious drops big news ahead of his highly anticipated UFC return
articles.description: Conor McGregor has confirmed he is satisfied with the UFC contract terms that paved the way for his long-awaited return at UFC 329 against Max Holloway. The former two-division champion revealed he finally feels “honored” by the promotion after complicated negotiations tied to the UFC’s new media rights era. Reports suggest McGregor still has only two fights remaining on his current deal, potentially setting up a major free-agency situation in combat sports. Alongside the contract news, McGregor also shared insight into his mindset entering the comeback.

articles.title: No place for Virat Kohli as Vaibhav Sooryavanshi gets the nod in former CSK star's best IPL 2026 playing XII
articles.description: Former India batter Subramaniam Badrinath picked his IPL 2026 league-stage best XII, leaving out Virat Kohli and KL Rahul. He chose Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Shubman Gill as openers, with Sai Sudharsan, Heinrich Klaasen, and Rajat Patidar in the batting lineup. His bowling-heavy squad included Krunal Pandya, Sunil Narine, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Jofra Archer, and Kagiso Rabada.
